<think>
Okay, so I need to figure out whether the mutant code change adjacent to line 91 in DateTimeZone.java is causing a fault. The mutant code was changed from checking if minutesOffset is between 0 and 59 to always throwing an IllegalArgumentException when true. 

Looking at the failing tests information, several tests are now failing because of this mutation. For example, testForOffsetHours_int in TestDateTimeZone started passing but then failed after mutation. Similarly, other tests like TestDateTimeFormatter's parse methods also failed.

The problem is that by removing the condition that checks if minutesOffset is within 0-59 and instead always throwing an exception when true doesn't make sense. Minutes should be between 0 and 59, so the original code correctly validates this range. The mutant incorrectly adds a condition that doesn't depend on the actual value of minutesOffset.

This means the mutation broke several tests because valid cases where minutes are within range are now being treated as errors. So yes, this mutation is causing adjacent faults.
</think>
